text.title: Graph RAG Indexing Pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the graph-rag-indexing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Chunk ids MUST be deterministic (sha1 of doc_id + chunk_index + content_hash), not random UUIDs.
rule.rationale: Resumable pipelines require idempotent re-runs; UUIDs break dedup on retry.

rule.statement: Run entity canonicalization (case fold + alias lookup + embedding-similarity dedup) BEFORE inserting nodes into the graph.
rule.rationale: Without canonicalization the graph explodes with duplicates ('OpenAI' / 'openai' / 'Open AI') and community detection breaks.

rule.statement: Community summaries MUST be cached keyed by (community_id, member_set_hash); regenerate only when membership changes.
rule.rationale: LLM summarization is the dominant cost item; regenerating per query burns budget.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST checkpoint progress per batch (extracted entities + chunk offset) to disk so a kill -9 resumes within one batch.
rule.rationale: Full restart of a 10k-doc indexing job costs hours and dollars; checkpointing reduces blast radius.

rule.statement: If corpus has no named entities OR queries are pure semantic-similarity, skip GraphRAG and use vanilla vector RAG.
rule.rationale: GraphRAG indexing is 10-50x cost of vanilla embedding; only pays off when traversal queries dominate.
